Defines macros for branch prediction optimization using `__builtin_expect`.

# Purpose
This C header file defines two macros, `LIKELY` and `UNLIKELY`, which are used to provide branch prediction hints to the compiler. The `LIKELY(x)` macro indicates that the expression `x` is expected to evaluate to true most of the time, while the `UNLIKELY(x)` macro suggests that the expression `x` is expected to evaluate to false most of the time. These macros use the `__builtin_expect` function, which is a GCC extension that helps optimize the performance of conditional branches by informing the compiler about the expected outcome of a condition.
